title: taeniatherum caput-medusae var. asperum
intro: Taeniatherum caput-medusae var. asperum, also known as rough-fruited blue wheatgrass, is a type of grass native to Mongolia and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, Taeniatherum caput-medusae var. asperum is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and may be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
